description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden cabinet featuring a rich, reddish-brown veneer and subtle gold trim. The front panel is dominated by a large, woven speaker grille above a horizontal frequency display, flanked by two prominent black tuning knobs. Below the display, there are several push buttons and a row of labeled controls, all set in a metallic gold panel. The sides of the cabinet have vertical black slatted vents, likely for sound or heat dissipation. The back panel is a flat, reddish-brown board with ventilation holes, labeled connectors, and printed technical information, including the model type and a crest logo. The top surface is flat and dark, suitable for placing small objects.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with a large front speaker grille, gold-trimmed controls, and side vents. The back panel features technical labels and connector ports.
description_view_0: The front view shows the woven speaker grille, frequency display, two black knobs, and a row of gold push buttons.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the wood finish and a vertical black slatted vent.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the reddish-brown back panel with ventilation holes, technical labels, and connector ports.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the wood finish and another vertical black slatted vent.
